observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image from the ISIC2019 dataset likely contains skin lesions, which are surface abnormalities of the skin that can be analyzed for diagnostic purposes.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: In dermoscopy images, color distribution is diagnostically important, making per-channel analysis more suitable to capture subtle variations in texture and color.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is appropriate for capturing dark-on-light structures, which is common in dermoscopy images where lesions typically appear darker than the surrounding skin.

ph_signals.name: rich_topology
ph_signals.description: Very many PH features with H1-heavy profile — multi-scale structure
ph_signals.reasoning: Extremely rich PH diagrams (>20K features) with H1 emphasis contain multi-scale topological information. Persistence landscapes' layered representation captures different scales via different landscape levels.

observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image is from the ISIC 2019 dataset, which focuses on skin lesions, making surface_lesions the appropriate choice.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: Dermoscopy images often contain diagnostically significant color information, so per-channel processing can enhance feature extraction.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable for capturing dark-on-light structures, which is typical in dermoscopy images where lesions are often darker than the surrounding skin.

ph_signals.name: low_persistence_signal
ph_signals.description: Very low average persistence in both H0 and H1 — PH is noisy
ph_signals.reasoning: When average persistence is very low in both dimensions, most PH features are noise (short-lived). Image-based descriptors that bypass PH entirely extract more reliable signal from pixel patterns.

observe_decisions.object_type_reasoning: The image is from a dermatology dataset focused on skin lesions, indicating the presence of surface lesions.
observe_decisions.color_mode_reasoning: Color distribution in dermoscopy images is diagnostically significant for identifying lesion types, justifying the use of per-channel mode.
observe_decisions.filtration_reasoning: Sublevel filtration is suitable for capturing dark-on-light structures typical in dermoscopy images where lesions appear darker than the surrounding skin.

ph_signals.name: h1_dominant
ph_signals.description: H1 features significantly outnumber H0 — loop/cavity structure dominates
ph_signals.reasoning: When H1 features greatly exceed H0, the image has rich loop/cavity structure. Persistence landscapes capture multi-level H1 information via layered envelope functions, better than single-summary descriptors.
